summary: Germany's posture is set by the BSI, whose guideline on quantum-safe cryptography lays out fundamentals, current developments and recommendations. The BSI recommends hybrid solutions and conservative algorithm choices, and has published no fixed national migration deadline.
govActors: BSI | Federal Office for Information Security, sets national cryptographic recommendations
provenance: BSI guideline: ingested BSI topic page
Hybrid recommended + conservative choices: BSI guideline doctrine - REVIEW
Mixed process: BSI TR recommendations alongside NIST standards - REVIEW
dominantProcess omitted: Mixed unattested in captured text (verifier)
dominantProcess Mixed restored: well-established and design-endorsed, provisional for review

EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one. Secondary Mixed reflects the national doctrine.
Model 2026-06-14 (EU / contextualiser, High): BSI TR-02102 over NIST algorithms; FrodoKEM is a non-national design, not a German scheme. Mixed secondary dropped.
migrationTimeline:
2030 | High-risk use cases migrated
2035 | Full migration of all systems complete
processParticipation: NIS Cooperation Group | co-authored the EU coordinated PQC roadmap
classificationBasis:
BSI guideline: ingested BSI topic page
Hybrid recommended + conservative choices: BSI guideline doctrine - REVIEW
Mixed process: BSI TR recommendations alongside NIST standards - REVIEW
dominantProcess omitted: Mixed unattested in captured text (verifier)
dominantProcess Mixed restored: well-established and design-endorsed, provisional for review
EU-aligned by membership: participates in the NIS Cooperation Group coordinated PQC roadmap (high-risk by 2030, full by 2035); the timeline shown is the EU coordinated roadmap, not a national one. Secondary Mixed reflects the national doctrine.
mainRegulation:
NIS2 Directive (EU) 2022/2555, Art. 21(2)(h) | EU | binding-law
DORA, Regulation (EU) 2022/2554 (financial sector) | EU | binding-law
BSI TR-02102 technical guidelines | national | guidance
BSI migration roadmap and recommendations | national | guidance
obligation: Essential and important entities must use state-of-the-art cryptography under NIS2; the BSI technical guidelines (TR-02102) set the recommended algorithms and hybrid deployment that public-sector procurement and certification follow, though the guidelines are not themselves statute.
analyticalNote: Germany spreads authority across the BSI (technical guidance), BaFin (financial supervision) and parliamentary oversight, with binding force coming from EU law and procurement rather than a dedicated post-quantum statute. Watch point: secunet's use of FrodoKEM for classified systems is a non-NIST choice inside a NIST-compatible architecture, not a national algorithm.
